_comment: TEMPLATE palette for cool-tone portraits (e.g. 2B from NieR:Automata) — needs pure white (dress), pure black (blindfold/hair), silver (sword blade), skin tone, and red (emblem). In 0.2.4 the white/silver/gray TODOs were FILLED with the Marble-серия. In 0.2.5 the black and red TODOs were FILLED with Small Coastal Stone (VLM-confirmed dark) and Maraket Rubble (VLM-confirmed reddish — user's intuition was right). Only the skin TODO remains: Sand Tussock's VLM RGB is inconsistent across measurements (KI-11), and no other known decoration is pale-tan enough. Until skin is filled, the palette LOADS (all non-TODO entries resolve to known hashes), but a 2B render will lack a believable face/hands tone.
_how_to_fill: 1. In the game, place ONE of each missing decoration in your hideout.
4. The 'Unknown hashes' section lists the hash for each unknown name.
5. Add the entry to KNOWN_HASHES in src/hideout_art/constants.py.
6. Add the decoration name to ART_TYPES in the same file (if it's purely artistic).
7. Replace the TODO entries below with the actual decoration name.

_rgb_sources._method: RGB values were measured via VLM (glm-4.6v) analysis of screenshots in исходники/. VLM identified each decoration visually and reported a mid-tone RGB. These are FIRST-PASS estimates, not pixel-exact medians — they may shift by ±10-15 points after a clean pixel sampling pass. See KI-11 in STATUS.md for the VLM-noise problem (Sand Tussock's RGB varied from (78,52,46) in 0.2.1 to (180,160,120) in 0.2.5 — likely different parts of the plant).
_rgb_sources.Cave Fossil: (140, 110, 80) — VLM mid-tone BROWN rock; previously assumed light gray — corrected in 0.2.4. NOT a 2B cool-palette candidate.
_rgb_sources.Cave Coral: (150, 130, 110) — VLM mid-tone brownish-gray; warm, not cool. NOT a 2B candidate.
_rgb_sources.Summit Brazier: (180, 140, 80) — VLM mid-tone golden-brown metal; warm, NOT cool. Candidate for warm 'red' role only.
_rgb_sources.Small Coastal Stone: (85, 75, 70) — VLM mid-tone DARK warm-gray, 0.2.5. is_dark=true, is_cool_tone=false (warm). Darkest non-plant decoration available. Used for 2B 'black' role (warmer than pure black, but recognisable as blindfold/hair).
_rgb_sources.Maraket Rubble: (153, 78, 68) — VLM mid-tone REDDISH-BROWN, 0.2.5. is_reddish=true. Prior 0.2.1 estimate (138,120,94) was too neutral — user correctly observed reddish tint. Used for 2B 'red' role (dark brick-red, darker than ideal but recognisable as emblem).
_rgb_sources.Falling Sand: (255, 192, 203) — VLM particle-effect PINK, 0.2.5 re-measured (matches 0.2.4 (248,187,208) within noise). Confirms NOT skin/red — too pink.
_rgb_sources.Seaweed: (128, 96, 64) — VLM mid-tone BROWN, 0.2.5 (new decoration in 0.2.5, hash 1015947674). Warm aquatic plant — NOT a 2B candidate. Documented for completeness.
_rgb_sources.Sand Tussock: VLM RGB CONFLICT — 0.2.1 (78,52,46) very dark brown vs 0.2.5 (180,160,120) light tan. Likely sampled different parts of the plant. NOT used for 'skin' until pixel sampling resolves the conflict (KI-11).

_colour_roles_needed_for_2b.white: 2B's dress, skin highlights, skirt trim. FILLED in 0.2.4 by Marble Fountain (230,230,220) — brightest marble, near-white. Marble Table (200,200,195) is the alternate mid-white.
_colour_roles_needed_for_2b.black: 2B's blindfold, hair, glove, boots. FILLED in 0.2.5 by Small Coastal Stone (85,75,70) — VLM-confirmed is_dark=true. Warmer than ideal pure black, but the darkest non-plant option. User intuition ('камни разные чем не черный?') was correct.
_colour_roles_needed_for_2b.gray: Shadows on dress, sword blade, metal details. FILLED in 0.2.4 by Marble Bench (210,210,205) and Marble Walls (210,210,205) — light cool gray.
_colour_roles_needed_for_2b.silver: Sword blade highlights, metallic accents. FILLED in 0.2.4 by Marble Table (200,200,195) — slightly cooler/darker than Marble Fountain, used for silver role. Note: 4 marble decorations have very similar RGB (200-230 range); this is intentional — they are 4 visually similar marble textures.
_colour_roles_needed_for_2b.skin: 2B's skin (visible near eyes/neck). STILL MISSING. Sand Tussock's VLM RGB is inconsistent (KI-11) — 0.2.1 measured (78,52,46), 0.2.5 measured (180,160,120), a 100+ point swing. Falling Sand (255,192,203) is too pink. Need a peach/tan decoration or pixel sampling of Sand Tussock to resolve which part is the mid-tone.
_colour_roles_needed_for_2b.pink: Glow accents, lips. COVERED by Falling Sand (255,192,203).
_colour_roles_needed_for_2b.red: Emblem on dress. FILLED in 0.2.5 by Maraket Rubble (153,78,68) — VLM-confirmed is_reddish=true. User correctly observed that Maraket Rubble has a reddish/pink tint. Prior candidate Summit Brazier (180,140,80) was golden-brown, too yellow — Maraket Rubble is much closer to true red.

_available_warm_alternatives_in_0_2_1._comment: These 0.2.1 RGB estimates are KNOWN TO BE NOISY (KI-11). The 0.2.5 VLM re-measurement of the Maraket-серия on укрошения.jpg showed substantial drift — Maraket Rubble went from (138,120,94) to (153,78,68), Maraket Treasures from (108,91,83) to (191,154,111). Treat 0.2.1 values here as placeholders pending re-measurement.
_available_warm_alternatives_in_0_2_1.Maraket Rubble: REDDISH-BROWN (153,78,68) — VLM 0.2.5 re-measured (was 138,120,94 in 0.2.1). Now fills the 2B 'red' role.
_available_warm_alternatives_in_0_2_1.Maraket Treasures: GOLD (191,154,111) — VLM 0.2.5 (was 108,91,83 in 0.2.1 — likely sampled shadow).
_available_warm_alternatives_in_0_2_1.Coastal Pebble: warm tan/khaki (134,115,94) — 0.2.1 estimate, not yet re-measured (KI-11).

_0_2_5_measured_rgb_summary._comment: VLM (glm-4.6v) re-analysis in 0.2.5 of three screenshots: водоросли и летающий песок.jpg (Seaweed + Falling Sand), камни и кустарники.jpg (Small Coastal Stone + Coastal Bush + Sand Tussock), укрошения.jpg (Maraket-серия). Closes 2 of 3 remaining 2B TODOs (black, red). User pushback on 0.2.4 conclusions was partially correct: Maraket Rubble IS reddish (not neutral tan as 0.2.1 said), and Small Coastal Stone IS dark enough for black. Sand Tussock RGB is too inconsistent to use for skin.

new_0_2_5_measurements.Seaweed: (128,96,64) — BROWN, warm. New decoration (hash 1015947674), NOT a 2B candidate.
new_0_2_5_measurements.Falling Sand: (255,192,203) — PINK, re-confirmed (matches 0.2.4 within noise). NOT skin/red.
new_0_2_5_measurements.Sand Tussock: (180,160,120) — light tan in 0.2.5 vs (78,52,46) dark brown in 0.2.1. CONFLICT — see KI-11.

todo_status_after_0_2_5.skin: STILL MISSING — pending pixel-sampling resolution of Sand Tussock RGB conflict (KI-11), or a new peach/tan decoration from another hideout.

entries._role: white (2B dress, skin highlights) — FILLED 0.2.4, VLM-measured from Marble Fountain
silver (sword blade, metallic accents) — FILLED 0.2.4, VLM-measured from Marble Table
gray (shadows on dress, sword details) — FILLED 0.2.4, VLM-measured from Marble Bench
gray alt (textural variety for dithered gradients) — FILLED 0.2.4, VLM-measured from Marble Walls
black (blindfold, hair) — FILLED 0.2.5, VLM-measured from Small Coastal Stone (is_dark=true)
skin tone (visible neck/face) — STILL MISSING. Sand Tussock RGB is inconsistent across VLM passes (KI-11); Falling Sand is too pink. Need a peach/tan decoration or pixel sampling.
red (emblem) — FILLED 0.2.5, VLM-measured from Maraket Rubble (is_reddish=true, user intuition confirmed)
